rules.message: Plain-http `http://` URL literal used from code that may run in an https-served app — browsers block this as mixed content, or otherwise silently downgrade that request to no TLS. Use https, or route through an env-based/configurable base URL for internal service hosts — the private-IP/localhost exclude on this rule does not cover hostnames that resolve internally without a literal private IP, or https-required internal proxies. Suppress a vetted case with `// mixed-content-ok`.

Committed localhost/private-IP endpoint — breaks outside the dev machine. Move it to env/config; if this file is genuinely dev-only, exclude it via config instead. Suppress a vetted case with `// localhost-ok`.

A GET request carrying a body (`method: 'get'` alongside `body:` in the same function) — servers/proxies may silently drop the body on a GET, so data the caller thinks it's sending never arrives. Use POST (or another body-bearing method) or move the data to query params. Suppress a vetted case with `// get-body-ok`.

WebSocket opened/upgraded (`new WebSocket(...)`/`.upgrade(...)`) with no auth material (`token`/`auth`/`session`/`cookie`/`jwt`) in the same function — an unauthenticated realtime channel lets anyone who can reach the socket connect. The WebSocket API can't set custom headers on the handshake, so instead use a short-lived one-time ticket/token in the WS URL's query string exchanged server-side, rely on same-origin cookies (sent automatically on the upgrade), or authenticate via the first message sent over the socket. Suppress a vetted case with `// ws-auth-ok`.
